label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. That would be February 28, 2001, right? Because 2001 is not a leap year. Leap years are every 4 years, like 2000, 2004, etc. So 2001 isn't a leap year, so February only has 28 days. Now, today is her 16-year-old birthday. That means today's date must be February 28, 2017. Because she was born in 2001, adding 16 years would make it 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. The question is asking for the date one year ago from today. So subtracting one year from today's date (02/28/2017) would take us to 02/28/2016. But wait, let me think again. If today is her birthday, then one year ago would be the same date last year, which is 2016. So the date should be 02/28/2016. Let me check the options. Option C is 02/28/2016. That seems correct. But wait, another way to think: if today is 02/28/2017, then one year ago would be exactly a year before. So the date would be 02/28/2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year. But that doesn't affect the calculation. Even if 2016 had a leap day, but since we're calculating the date a year before, the actual date is February 28, which exists in both 2016 and 2017. Looking at the options, option C is 02/28/2016. That should be the correct answer. Let me check the other options. Option A is 02/28/2015. That would be two years ago from 2017, not
